$comment:
c85 (shell.c `atexit(abnormalExit)`): the per-format MACRO/SYMBOL SPLIT (the errno.json pattern, pure config, zero engine change). elf: glibc exports no dynamic `atexit` (import fails at LOAD, witnessed c82) -> `atexit(f)` is a function-like macro (C 7.1.4p1) expanding to `__cxa_atexit((void (*)(void *))(f), 0, 0)` — glibc's own shim semantics (dso_handle=NULL = main-program registration); __cxa_atexit is the import (T, single-version, both arches). pe: no macro variant (0-match => un-injected) — `atexit` is the direct msvcrt.dll export (objdump-verified [1068]); ExitProcess runs msvcrt's onexit table at its CRT DLL-detach. THE RUN PROVES THE HANDLER FIRES AT EXIT: main writes+flushes its marker then returns 42 (≡ exit(42), C 5.1.2.2.3); exit runs handlers BEFORE stream close (7.22.4.4) so the handler's line lands second; the handler prints ONLY if it observed main's last write (mainRan==1) — the strict two-line stdout + exit-42 assertion fails on a dropped, early, or spurious registration. macho: the DIRECT `atexit` symbol — Darwin's libSystem exports `_atexit` (c117; stdlib.json gates atexit [pe,macho] on the SYMBOL, elf-only macro stays 0-match/un-injected on macho), so `atexit(f)` resolves to libSystem `_atexit`; LF stdout (no msvcrt text-mode 
). RED-ON-DISABLE witnessed: drop the macro (elf) / the symbol (pe) -> S0001 `got atexit`. gcc -std=c17 cross-checked in WSL (exit 42, identical stdout). Line endings follow the adjacent_string_concat convention: msvcrt's text-mode stdout emits 
, so the manifest-level pin is the pe form and each elf target overrides with the 
 form.
